title: eupatorium rotundifolium var. scabridum
intro: Eupatorium rotundifolium var. scabridum, also known as scabrid eupatorium or rough-leaved joe-pye weed, is a plant species in the Asteraceae family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, this plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as arthritis, fever, and wounds. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Eupatorium rotundifolium var. scabridum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for topical or internal use, although its safety and efficacy should be verified through scientific research and medical consultation.
